title: Understanding Depression: Beyond 'Just Feeling Sad'
titleAr: فهم الاكتئاب: أبعد من 'مجرد الشعور بالحزن'
learningObjective: After this exercise, the user will be able to differentiate normal sadness from depression, identify key DSM-5-TR criteria (educational), and challenge 3 common myths.
learningObjectiveAr: بعد هذا التمرين، سيتمكن المستخدم من التفريق بين الحزن الطبيعي والاكتئاب.

content.scenario:
Depression is NOT 'just being sad.' According to WHO (2023), depression is characterized by:
- Persistent low mood or loss of interest (anhedonia) lasting 2+ weeks
- Accompanied by: changes in sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, or self-worth
- Significant impairment in daily functioning

**Critical distinctions:**
- Sad ≠ Depressed (sadness is temporary; depression is persistent)
- Lazy ≠ Depressed (motivation loss is a SYMPTOM, not a choice)
- Strong ≠ Not Depressed (depression affects everyone regardless of 'strength')

task.instructions: Read each scenario and determine whether it describes normal sadness or may indicate something requiring further attention (NOT diagnosis).
task.instructionsAr: اقرأ كل سيناريو وحدد ما إذا كان يصف حزناً طبيعياً.

items.text: A student feels very sad for 3 days after a breakup but gradually starts feeling better. → Normal sadness (proportionate, temporary)
items.explanation: Normal sadness. The sadness is proportionate to the event, temporary (3 days), and shows a trajectory of improvement. This is a healthy emotional response.

items.text: A student has lost interest in ALL activities for 4 weeks, sleeps 14 hours/day, and can't attend classes. → May indicate something requiring attention
items.explanation: This pattern (duration 4+ weeks, pervasive loss of interest, sleep changes, functional impairment) suggests more than normal sadness. Encouraging professional consultation would be appropriate.

items.text: A student says 'I'm depressed' because they failed an exam, but is laughing with friends an hour later. → Likely colloquial use, not clinical depression
items.explanation: Using 'depressed' colloquially is common but inaccurate. Clinical depression is persistent (2+ weeks) and pervasive. This appears to be temporary disappointment.

feedback.correct: Excellent mental health literacy! You can now distinguish between normal sadness and patterns that may need professional attention. Remember: recognition is NOT diagnosis.
feedback.correctAr: ممتاز! يمكنك الآن التمييز بين الحزن الطبيعي والأنماط التي قد تحتاج اهتماماً مهنياً.
feedback.incorrect: Review the key distinctions: duration (temporary vs. persistent), scope (specific vs. pervasive), and impairment (functioning vs. not functioning).

whatNotToDo: Don't diagnose — only trained professionals can diagnose depression
Don't tell someone with depression to 'cheer up' — this dismisses their experience
safetyNote: This is educational content. If you or someone you know shows persistent signs, please encourage consultation with a mental health professional. Egypt crisis line: 16328.
com_b_mechanism: Differentiating sadness from depression builds mental health recognition capability
